facts.statement: Roman aqueducts carried water from distant sources into cities using gravity alone.
questions: How did Roman aqueducts transport water?
What power source did Roman aqueducts use?
How did Romans move water from distant sources to cities?

facts.statement: Water in Roman aqueducts flowed down a gradual slope along channels of stone and concrete.
questions: What material were Roman aqueduct channels made of?
How did water flow through Roman aqueducts?
What was the construction of Roman aqueduct channels?

facts.statement: The Aqua Appia was the first Roman aqueduct.
questions: What was the first Roman aqueduct?
Which aqueduct was built first by the Romans?
What is the name of the earliest Roman aqueduct?

facts.statement: The Aqua Appia was built in 312 BCE.
questions: When was the Aqua Appia built?
What year was the first Roman aqueduct constructed?
In what year did Romans build the Aqua Appia?

facts.statement: Romans built arched bridges where valleys had to be crossed to keep the aqueduct channel at the correct gentle gradient.
questions: Why did Romans build arched bridges for aqueducts?
How did Romans maintain aqueduct gradient across valleys?
What did Romans use to cross valleys with aqueducts?

facts.statement: At its height, the city of Rome was served by eleven aqueducts.
questions: How many aqueducts served Rome at its height?
What was the number of aqueducts in Rome at its peak?
How many aqueducts supplied ancient Rome?

facts.statement: Rome's eleven aqueducts supplied public baths, fountains, and private homes.
questions: What did Rome's aqueducts supply water to?
What facilities were served by Roman aqueducts?
Where did the water from Rome's aqueducts go?
